summary: Runtime dependency pinned to mutable GitHub master-branch tarball
details: package.json declares the package's sole runtime dependency, `node-fetch-core`, as a GitHub archive URL pointing at the mutable `master` branch (`https://github.com/Hexa-devy/node-fetch-core/archive/refs/heads/master.tar.gz`) with no commit SHA, tag, or version pin. On every `npm install`, npm fetches whatever the current contents of that branch are and installs them as the dependency. The repository owner can change the tarball contents at any time — including adding `preinstall`/`postinstall` lifecycle scripts and rewriting the code loaded by `require('node-fetch-utils')` — without publishing a new version of node-fetch-utils to the npm registry. This bypasses registry review, sidesteps semver-based version locking, and means the code that actually runs on installer machines is not the code reviewable from the npm tarball. Any downstream installer of node-fetch-utils@1.2.4 effectively grants arbitrary future code-execution rights on their build/install machines to the owner of the Hexa-devy/node-fetch-core repository.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
